seoTitle: How to Set Up 301 Redirects with Cloudflare Rules
metaDescription: Set up a permanent 301 redirect in Cloudflare using Page Rules or the newer Redirect Rules engine, with wildcard matching and path preservation.
intro: Cloudflare offers two ways to forward URLs permanently: the legacy Page Rules interface and the newer Redirect Rules engine, and knowing which to use — and how to write the matching pattern — makes the difference between a clean redirect and a broken one. This guide covers both paths, including wildcard matching and preserving deep page paths during the forward.

steps.heading: Set up a proxied placeholder record
steps.body: Make sure the hostname you're redirecting has a proxied (orange cloud) DNS record, since Cloudflare's rules only apply to traffic passing through its edge.

steps.heading: Choose your redirect method
steps.body: Decide between a traditional Page Rule or the modern Single Redirect rule, both found under the Rules section of your dashboard.

steps.heading: Build a Page Rule redirect
steps.body: Create a Page Rule, enter your source URL pattern using wildcards where needed, and select the Forwarding URL setting for the action.

steps.heading: Or build a Redirect Rule
steps.body: Use the modern Redirect Rules expression builder to match incoming requests with custom filter expressions instead of simple wildcards.

steps.heading: Map the destination with path variables
steps.body: Use dynamic replacements like `${1}` in your destination URL to preserve the original page path when forwarding wildcard matches.

steps.heading: Set the status code and deploy
steps.body: Choose "301 – Permanent Redirect," decide whether to preserve the query string, then deploy and test the live links.

faq.question: Should I use Page Rules or Redirect Rules?
faq.answer: Redirect Rules is the newer, more flexible engine and is generally recommended; Page Rules still works but is being phased toward legacy status.

faq.question: Why isn't my redirect working even though the rule looks correct?
faq.answer: Check that the source DNS record is proxied (orange cloud) — Cloudflare rules only fire on traffic that passes through its network.

faq.question: What's the difference between a 301 and a 302 redirect?
faq.answer: A 301 signals a permanent move and is best for SEO when a URL is changing for good; a 302 signals a temporary redirect.

faq.question: Can I preserve query strings during a redirect?
faq.answer: Yes, both Page Rules and Redirect Rules include a toggle to preserve the original query string when forwarding.
